summary: This notebook demonstrates a baseline regression pipeline for predicting five polymer physical properties (Tg, FFV, Tc, Density, Rg) from SMILES strings using RDKit-derived molecular descriptors and LightGBM. It automates the extraction of approximately 192 chemical features, handles missing values via median imputation, and trains five independent LightGBM regressors via 5-fold cross-validation. The author emphasizes that RDKit handles the heavy lifting of feature generation, while the ML model simply learns the mapping to the targets. Readers should take away a reproducible, efficient recipe for cheminformatics-based regression, including how to estimate the competition metric locally and average CV fold predictions for inference.
competition_flow: Loads train and test CSVs containing SMILES strings, extracts ~192 RDKit molecular descriptors, imputes missing values, scales features, trains five independent LightGBM models via 5-fold CV for each of the five target properties, averages the fold predictions, and generates a submission CSV with an estimated local LB score.
data_reading: pd.read_csv loads train.csv and test.csv from Kaggle input paths; the SMILES column is extracted as a numpy array for processing.
data_processing: Replaces infinite values with NaN, imputes remaining NaNs with column medians, drops descriptors with >98% missing values, and applies StandardScaler normalization to the feature matrix.
features_engineering: Auto-discovers valid RDKit Descriptors functions, filters AUTOCORR2D descriptors to the lowest 10 by numeric suffix, retains all other valid descriptors (~192 total), and drops features with >98% missingness across the dataset.
ensembling: Averages predictions from the five cross-validation fold models for each target to produce the final prediction.
insights: RDKit molecular descriptors provide a strong, automated feature set for polymer property regression without manual engineering.
Training separate models per target accommodates differing data distributions and missingness patterns.
Median imputation is sufficient for handling descriptor calculation failures.
Averaging CV fold predictions stabilizes inference and reduces variance.
critical_findings: Only the FFV target has substantial non-missing training data, while other targets contain significant label sparsity.
RDKit descriptor computation can fail silently for certain SMILES strings, requiring explicit try/except and NaN handling.
The locally estimated LB score may be optimistic due to potential train/test distribution shifts.
